beats.n_start: you run cargo that pays no toll through the black canals of {CITY}; tonight the {TARGET} tallies the season and its {VAULT_NAME} stands open until the {DEADLINE_SIGN}, one night only; inside waits {GOAL}, a strongbox of coin and the {LEDGER} that names every runner in the city; {COMPANION} the boatman idles in the reeds below the {START_LANDMARK}; establish the stakes and the single clock, the {DEADLINE_SIGN}
beats.n_quay: {COMPANION} poles you toward the drop and gives the warnings you will live or die by, spoken plain: the tide turns at the {DEADLINE} and after it the low sluices and cuts drown a person in a breath; the {ROUTE_C_NAME} is watched every night by {WATCHERS} who miss nothing; the {TARGET} keeps a {YD_HAZARD} that will not bark; and never fight the current, it keeps whoever fights it; he waits at the {RENDEZVOUS} until the {DEADLINE_SIGN}, no later
beats.n_drop: you go over the side into the cold and {COMPANION} is gone into the reeds behind you, the skiff a shadow among shadows; the {TARGET} stands off across the black water, a single lit window high in it, the season's whole take waiting behind a {LOCK_NAME} inside; you fix the shape of it against the sky, count the bridges once more, and start the long quiet swim toward its walls
beats.n_approach: three ways lead from the drop to the {TARGET} {GATE_NAME}; you weigh them in the dark; the open {ROUTE_A_NAME} is fast but runs under the quay lamps; the flooded {ROUTE_B_NAME} is quiet though the tide is climbing inside it; and the {ROUTE_C_NAME} crosses over the top, the watched bridge {COMPANION} named, {WATCHERS} on it every night; restate each danger plainly
beats.ap_cut1: you keep to the shadow of the moored hulls in the {ROUTE_A_NAME}, breathing slow; a quay lamp sweeps the water and passes; ahead the iron {GATE_NAME} waits, but a low lighter is moored across the lane
beats.ap_cut2: the lighter blocks the lane; two ways past it; under its hull is a breath-held glide through the dark, the way {COMPANION} taught, trusting the water; around it means out into the open where the quay lamp has a clear sweep of the lane and will find you
beats.ap_cut_caught: the lamp catches you full in the open lane and holds you; a whistle goes up along the quay and is answered; there is no shadow to reach before the watch closes in, and they close in; the job ends here, in their hands
beats.ap_loft1: the {ROUTE_B_NAME} is waist-deep and rising, rotten canvas hanging like weed, a single lantern swinging that you must not knock; the water is colder than the canal and it is coming up; two ways cross the loft ahead
beats.ap_loft2: far off the {DEADLINE} begins to toll, and {COMPANION}'s warning rings with it, the low ways drown after the turn; the high beam walk runs dry above the flood, slow but sound; the low sluice runs fast and level but it is a low way and the bell is tolling now
beats.ap_loft_drown: the last stroke of the {DEADLINE} falls and the sluice fills in a heartbeat, just as {COMPANION} said it would; the current takes you and does not give you back; you fought the water, and the water kept you
beats.ap_bridge: you are barely onto the {ROUTE_C_NAME} when the {WATCHERS} turn as one; a bridge has no shadow and no water to drop into that they cannot watch you fall; they take you between them without hurry; {COMPANION} warned you, and the bridge was exactly what he said
beats.wg_gate: the {TARGET} {GATE_NAME}, an iron portcullis half-drowned in the canal, entry-agnostic to whichever lane brought you; the {WG_CHAIN_NAME} is fat with rust; the bars are wide enough to slip under if you go beneath the water; and a wicket door sits above the tide-line with a latch that has never sat true
beats.wg_under1: you go under, the cold closing over your head, the bars sliding past; but an old net is snagged across the gap below, invisible until it is around your arm and holding
beats.wg_under2: the net holds your arm underwater and your air is going; {COMPANION}'s last warning is the one that matters now, panic is what drowns people here; your knife is at your hip; or you can wrench and thrash for the surface and hope the net tears
beats.wg_under_drown: you thrash and the net only draws tighter, the way a snare does; the air goes out of you in a silver rope; you fought the water instead of the knot, and {COMPANION} told you how that ends
beats.wg_wicket1: the wicket's latch lifts with patience and a thin blade, quiet as a held breath; you fold through the gap dry-shouldered into the shadow beyond the gate, the far door of the yard just ahead
beats.wg_chain1: the {WG_CHAIN_NAME} will move the gate, but rust means noise, and noise this close to the house is a bell of its own; you can ease it link by patient link, or haul it fast and be through before anyone places the sound
beats.wg_alarm: the chain screeches once, short and loud, and you are through and flattened in the yard shadow before it dies; no one comes at a run, but somewhere inside the house a floorboard answers, and the night has teeth now; you move on, wary
beats.yard: the {TARGET} yard, walled and wet, entry-agnostic to how you came through the gate; near the far door a {YD_HAZARD} sits chained and silent, the dog that does not bark that {COMPANION} named, watching you with pale eyes; three ways lead inward; a {YD_COAL_NAME} drops into the cellars; a clerks' stair climbs to a lit landing; and the main door lies past the {YD_HAZARD}, whose chain runs just short of the wall
beats.yd_coal1: the {YD_COAL_NAME} drops you into a black cellar that smells of dust and river; you find your feet among the coal and grope for a way up and in; an old iron grate and a steep coal-chute offer themselves
beats.yd_coal2: the grate is locked but the lock is old and honest and will pick; the coal-chute is faster but it drops away blind into a dark you cannot sound, and a blind drop in a strange house can end anywhere, on stone or on nothing
beats.yd_coal_trap: the chute spills you into a dry cistern with walls too smooth and too high to climb, no coal, no rope, no way up; you are not hurt and you are not caught, but you are held, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} will ring with you still down here; the job is over
beats.yd_stair1: the clerks' stair climbs toward a landing where a candle burns; a {YD_CLERK_NAME} is awake up here, closer than you would like, his shadow moving on the wall as you reach the top step
beats.yd_stair2: the clerk sits at his ledgers with his back half to you; wait in the stairwell dark and he will carry his candle off on his round soon enough; or slip past behind him now while he writes, close enough to touch, betting he does not turn
beats.yd_stair_caught: you are a stride behind him when the floor speaks under your foot; he turns, and his shout is up before your hand can reach him; the house wakes around you and there is nowhere in a lit room to go; they have you
beats.yd_dog1: you edge along the yard wall toward the far door, and the silent {YD_HAZARD} rises to the length of its chain, pale eyes tracking you, no sound at all from it, which is somehow worse than any growl; you measure the reach of the chain against the door
beats.yd_dog2: the {YD_HAZARD} will not bark, but it will bite, and its chain reaches almost to the door; hug the wall and its arc falls just short, a hand's breadth of safe stone the whole way; cross straight and quick and you are inside its reach the entire crossing, trusting it to be slow
beats.yd_dog_bite: it comes off its haunches without a sound and it is not slow; the chain snaps taut a heartbeat too late to save you; no one in the house hears a thing, which is the whole terrible point of a dog that does not bark
beats.hall: the counting-hall, entry-agnostic to which way you came inside; long tables of ledgers, iron strongboxes, and set in the far wall behind a clerk's cage the {VAULT_NAME} door itself; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is not far now; three ways cross to it; the open counting-floor runs straight but a {HL_GUARD_NAME}'s lantern crosses it on a slow round; the {HL_STACK_NAME} give cover along the wall; and a {HL_MEZZ_NAME} ladder climbs to the {VAULT_NAME}'s upper hatch, the long way round
beats.hl_floor: the {HL_GUARD_NAME}'s lantern crosses the open floor and returns on a slow count you can measure; go on the dark of his turn and the floor is yours for a handful of heartbeats; go now, while his light still rakes the boards, and you cross lit, in the open, counting on speed you do not have
beats.hl_floor_caught: his light swings back before you are halfway and pins you on the open floor with nowhere to fold into; the {HL_GUARD_NAME} does not even raise his voice, only his lantern and a hand; the counting-floor was never a place to hurry
beats.hl_stack1: the {HL_STACK_NAME} tower along the wall, years of the {TARGET} piled into paper cliffs; you move in their cover toward the cage, and the way pinches between a leaning stack and the wall
beats.hl_stack2: the leaning stack sways at a touch, a slow paper avalanche waiting; squeeze low underneath it and you pass beneath the sway; climb over the top and your weight is the one thing it cannot hold, and a fallen stack is a drum the whole house will hear
beats.hl_stack_fall: the stack goes over with a roar of paper and you go down with it, unhurt but loud, so loud; boots wake and turn toward the sound; you are up and running the wrong way, out and empty, the {HAUL_NAME} left behind you in a house that is now awake
beats.hl_mezz1: the {HL_MEZZ_NAME} ladder takes you up above the counting-floor into rafters and dust, the long way to the {VAULT_NAME}'s upper hatch; you cross a catwalk of old boards toward the hatch above the door
beats.hl_mezz2: the upper hatch gives onto a short iron ladder that drops you straight down the inside of the cage to the {VAULT_NAME} door itself; you take it rung by rung and set your feet at the foot of the door
beats.vlanding: the clerk's cage stands before the {VAULT_NAME}, its gate the last thing between you and the door; entry-agnostic to which way crossed the hall; the gate is chained loosely, pickable, or you can go around by the long counter where a tray of counted coin has been left out overnight
beats.land_counter: the counter runs the long way to the door, and on it sits the {LAND_COIN_NAME}, silver already counted and stacked, close enough to sweep into a pocket; greed is noise, though, and a sweep of loose coin is a sound; leave it and move quiet, or take it and gamble the sound against the reward
beats.land_greed: the loose silver chimes as it goes, one clear bright sound in a silent house, and one sound is all it takes; the cage answers, then the hall; you had the {VAULT_NAME} a step away and you spent it on a handful of counted coin
beats.land_cross: the last stretch of floor to the {VAULT_NAME}, quiet now, the cage behind you; you set your hands on the great door and feel the old cold iron of it, and the {LOCK_NAME} waiting in its heart
beats.g_vault: the {VAULT_NAME} door, and only the door; a {CITY} {LOCK_NAME} built to be read, not forced, three wards that must turn in their season, and behind it {GOAL}, the strongbox and the {LEDGER}; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is the true clock and it is close; describe only the door, the lock, the wards, and the prize within, nothing of the room behind you or the way you came
beats.vk_work1: the first ward gives to a patient hand, a soft iron sigh, the {LOCK_NAME} reading true under your fingers; the second ward is the one with teeth, and you feel it waiting
beats.vk_work2: the second ward is a false gate, and a {CITY} false gate bites the hand that hurries it, snapping shut for good on too much force; you can set it gently, feeling for its true seat, or turn it hard and trust to strength, which is the one thing it is built to punish
beats.vk_snap: the false gate snaps shut under your force with a small final click, and the {LOCK_NAME} seizes solid, dead for the night and the season; no alarm, no hand on your shoulder, only a door that will never open now; you leave the way you came, empty
beats.vk_force: the pry-bar bites the door's edge, and through the steel you feel a thin wire tighten, the inner bell-wire, one more pound of force from ringing the house awake; ease off now and go back to picking the wards by hand, or throw your full weight on the bar and take the door before the bell can matter
beats.vk_force_bell: the bar wins the last inch and the bell-wire wins the last heartbeat; a bell rings deep inside the door and the whole house rings after it; the {VAULT_NAME} is open and you are standing in front of it as the boots arrive; you forced what was meant to be read
beats.vk_ledger: the {LEDGER} holds the door's cipher written plain, but a {CITY} lock turns its false way first and its true way second, and the ledger gives only the numbers, not the order; trust the true turn against the plain reading, or turn it the plain way the ledger seems to say
beats.vk_ledger_lock: you turn it the plain way and the {LOCK_NAME} takes the wrong order as a refusal and seizes, locking itself for the season; the numbers were true and the order was everything, and the ledger never told you the order; the door stays shut, and you go
beats.vk_open: the {LOCK_NAME} sighs its whole length and the {VAULT_NAME} swings; inside, the strongbox of coin and the {LEDGER}, {GOAL} entire, every runner's name and the season's silver in your hands at last; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is very close now, and a full {VAULT_NAME} is only half the night; you take the {HAUL_NAME} and turn for the water
beats.vk_secure1: you swing the {VAULT_NAME} to and reset the outer ward behind you, so the theft will read as a locked door until someone tries it come morning, buying yourself the whole of the escape before the house even knows; the {HAUL_NAME} is heavy and whole against your chest
beats.vk_secure2: back across the counting-hall you go, keeping low along the tables, the {HAUL_NAME} a dead weight now that every sense is bent on the way out; you reach the head of the routes back to the water with the {DEADLINE_SIGN} nearer than you would like
beats.h_escape: the {HAUL_NAME} is yours and now the night is only the way out, before the {DEADLINE_SIGN} and before {COMPANION} gives up the {RENDEZVOUS}; the tide stands high now, which changes every low way; three routes back to the water; the {GATE_NAME} you came by, though the tide has risen over its bars; the {YD_COAL_NAME} drops to a fast side canal; and the {ES_ROOF_NAME} climb high and exposed over the whole quarter
beats.es_gate1: the {GATE_NAME} again, but the risen tide has closed over the bars where you slipped under before, no air gap left below now; only the high wicket still stands dry above the flood
beats.es_gate2: the wicket is dry and slow and sure; the drowned bars are fast and the way you know, but the tide has taken the air from beneath them and {COMPANION}'s warning stands, the low ways drown after the turn, and the turn is long past
beats.es_gate_drown: you go under expecting the gap that let you in and there is no gap now, only the risen tide packed solid against iron; you turn back and there is no air behind you either; the tide closed the door you came through, exactly when {COMPANION} said it would
beats.es_side1: the {YD_COAL_NAME} lets you out above a side canal running hard and loud with the turned tide, a {ES_RACE_NAME} funneling the whole flood through the narrows; a footbridge crosses above it, and the fast black water runs below
beats.es_side2: the footbridge is a plank and sound; the race below is faster and would carry you to the basin in a breath, but a {ES_RACE_NAME} drowns whatever it carries, you have seen it, it does not let go until the narrows are past and by then the person it holds is past too
beats.es_side_drown: the race takes you the instant you give yourself to it and it does not carry, it holds; the narrows come and go and you do not; the water reaches the basin without you; you knew what it did and you gave yourself to it anyway
beats.es_roof1: the {ES_ROOF_NAME} are wet slate and steep, the whole quarter black below, the canals threading silver four floors down; you work along the ridge toward a drainpipe that climbs down into shadow
beats.es_roof2: the drainpipe is old but bolted and it will hold you down the wall to a ledge above the water; the other way is a jump to the next roof, a gap that clears nothing but open air and, four floors below it, the black canal that will not care how well you jumped
beats.es_roof_fall: your foot leaves the wet slate and you know at once the gap was longer than the dark let it look; there is only air, and then the water, four floors down and hard as a floor from that height; the jump cleared nothing
beats.junction: the canal junction where every way to the {RENDEZVOUS} comes together, entry-agnostic to which route reached the water; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} trembles near now, minutes not hours; {COMPANION}'s skiff must be just past the far arch; three last ways lie open; a {JN_REED_NAME} hides quiet along the bank; the open basin runs straight and fast under the last {JN_WATCHPOST_NAME}; and the smugglers cut itself, the low pipe under the road that gives the run its name, though the tide is filling it
beats.jn_reed: the {JN_REED_NAME} is slow and silent and long, the reeds closing over you as you go, the {JN_WATCHPOST_NAME}'s light never once finding the bank; it draws you the long quiet way around the basin's edge
beats.jn_reed2: the reeds thin at last and the black shape of the {RENDEZVOUS} leans out over the water just ahead, {COMPANION}'s skiff somewhere in its shadow; you cover the last stretch of open bank low and slow
beats.jn_basin: the open basin is the fast way and the last {JN_WATCHPOST_NAME} stands over it with a bell and a clear flat shot of the whole water; keep to the shadowed rim and the crossing is long but dark; cut straight across the open and it is quick and lit and watched every stroke
beats.jn_basin_caught: you are three strokes into the open when the post's bell starts and does not stop; the flat water gives you nowhere and the watch has boats of its own; they run you down before the far side; the shadowed rim was right there, the whole slow safe length of it
beats.jn_cut: the low pipe runs under the road to the {RENDEZVOUS}, the smugglers cut, and the tide is filling it fast, one clear run left in it before it drowns; run it now and beat the water by a breath; or wait for the patrol crossing the road above to pass first and give the tide that breath instead
beats.jn_cut_drown: the patrol passes and you drop into the pipe a breath too late; the tide has taken the last of the air and filled the cut wall to wall; there is no room to turn in a pipe and no air to turn for; the run that named the whole job is the run that keeps you
beats.boat: the {RENDEZVOUS}, and {COMPANION}'s skiff low in its shadow, oars shipped and ready, entry-agnostic to which last way brought you; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is a breath from ringing; the {HAUL_NAME} is heavy and whole against your chest; but out on the black water a watch-skiff has nosed into the basin, not yet turned your way; the choice now is only how much of the night you carry out of it
beats.e_clean: you are into the skiff and {COMPANION} has you off the {RENDEZVOUS} before the watch-skiff finishes its slow turn; the reeds take you, then the dark, then the wide safe water below the city; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} rings behind you over a {TARGET} that does not yet know its {VAULT_NAME} is empty; the strongbox and the {LEDGER} ride whole at your feet, {GOAL} entire, every name and every coin, carried clean out of {CITY} in the last dark of the night
beats.e_cost: the watch-skiff turns and you make your one hard choice, the strongbox over the side to lighten the skiff, its splash the price of the rowing that follows; {COMPANION} pulls and you pull and the reeds swallow you before the watch can close; the silver is gone to the canal but the {LEDGER} rides against your chest, every runner's name safe out of the {TARGET}, which was always the {HAUL_NAME} that counted; you carry the true prize home and leave the watch the coin's cold splash
beats.e_walkaway: the watch-skiff swings its lantern toward the {RENDEZVOUS} and you do the last thing a runner ever wants to, the whole {HAUL_NAME} into the black water, strongbox and ledger both, gone where no search will find them tonight; when the watch draws alongside you are only a boatman and his mate on the dawn tide with nothing to hold; they let you go because there is nothing to hold you for; you carry out no silver and no names, only your life and {COMPANION}'s, empty-handed and alive and free to run again
beats.m1_p0a: the turning narrows and darkens, old brick weeping white salt, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p0b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p0e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_p1a: the turning narrows and darkens, coils of rope gone to slime, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p1b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p1e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_h1: deeper into the {TARGET} cellars, and it keeps giving you more of itself, a low brick vaulting furred with saltpetre; two dead turns and one way on offer themselves, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is not waiting for you to choose
beats.m1_p2a: the turning narrows and darkens, cold water round tumbled coppers, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p2b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p2e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_p3a: the turning narrows and darkens, a dark that swallows your hand, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p3b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p3e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_h2: deeper into the {TARGET} cellars, and it keeps giving you more of itself, a store of rotted rope and broken tide-gear; two dead turns and one way on offer themselves, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is not waiting for you to choose
beats.m1_p4a: the turning narrows and darkens, a round room throwing your breath back, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p4b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p4e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_p5a: the turning narrows and darkens, bare shelves and standing damp, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p5b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p5e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_h3: deeper into the {TARGET} cellars, and it keeps giving you more of itself, a sunken laundry half-full of standing water; two dead turns and one way on offer themselves, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is not waiting for you to choose
beats.m1_p6a: the turning narrows and darkens, a stair walled off at its top, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p6b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p6e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_p7a: the turning narrows and darkens, a low gallery ankle-deep and going nowhere, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p7b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p7e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_h4: deeper into the {TARGET} cellars, and it keeps giving you more of itself, a coal-black passage that forgets which way is up; two dead turns and one way on offer themselves, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is not waiting for you to choose
beats.m1_p8a: the turning narrows and darkens, a dead iron furnace and its ash, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p8b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p8e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_p9a: the turning narrows and darkens, bin after bin of black coal, all alike, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p9b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p9e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_h5: deeper into the {TARGET} cellars, and it keeps giving you more of itself, a cistern room where your own breath comes back at you; two dead turns and one way on offer themselves, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is not waiting for you to choose
beats.m1_p10a: the turning narrows and darkens, stone that runs with the canal behind it, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p10b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p10e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_p11a: the turning narrows and darkens, a way that makes you crawl and then stops, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p11b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p11e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_h6: deeper into the {TARGET} cellars, and it keeps giving you more of itself, a warren of pantries emptied and left to the damp; two dead turns and one way on offer themselves, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is not waiting for you to choose
beats.m1_p12a: the turning narrows and darkens, a black shaft of standing water, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you
beats.m1_p12b: it leads you on and down and gives you nothing but more of the {TARGET} cellars, the way back already unsure
beats.m1_p12e: the way simply ends, a blank wall or a drowned door or a locked dark, no vault and no water beyond it; you are not caught and not hurt, only lost and out of night; the {DEADLINE_SIGN} finds you here and the job is done, unfinished
beats.m1_p13a: the turning narrows and darkens, seized winches and snapped chain, the sounds of the house or the water fading behind you

beats.m3_pfin_b: it narrows and turns once more and the light and the sound of the world are gone behind you now
beats.m3_pfin_e: the warren turns you and turns you until every dark rio looks like the last, and the {RENDEZVOUS} is a place you can no longer find; then the {DEADLINE_SIGN} rings out over the water and {COMPANION}, true to his word, gives up the {RENDEZVOUS} and is gone; you are alive and free and adrift with the {HAUL_NAME} and nowhere left to carry it tonight
beats.m3_h9: deeper into the canal warren, and it keeps giving you more of itself, a tide-flat going out to mud and no channel at all; two dead turns and one way on offer themselves, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} is not waiting for you to choose

titles.yd_dog_bite: The Dog That Does Not Bark
titles.vk_ledger_lock: What the Ledger Did Not Say

n_start.c_n_start_1: Climb down to {COMPANION} and the skiff.

n_approach.c_n_approach_1: Run the open {ROUTE_A_NAME}, low and fast.

yard.c_yard_3: Cross past the silent {YD_HAZARD} to the main door.

yd_dog2.c_yd_dog2_1: Hug the wall past the {YD_HAZARD}'s chain.

g_vault.c_g_vault_1: Read the {LOCK_NAME} and work the wards in turn.
g_vault.c_g_vault_3: Try the {LEDGER} for the door's cipher first.

vk_open.c_vk_open_1: Turn for the water with the {HAUL_NAME}.

h_escape.c_h_escape_2: Out the {YD_COAL_NAME} to the side canal.

boat.c_boat_1: Board and push off with the whole {HAUL_NAME}.
